skill.name: clarify
skill.description: Improve unclear UX copy, error messages, microcopy, labels, and instructions. Makes interfaces easier to understand and use.
skill.summary: Improve UX copy so users understand interface text and know what to do next.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives in plain UX-writing prose. The skill contains guidance for improving interface copy and does not include executable code, network access, file access, secret handling, or malicious prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Matches
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged prose in SKILL.md as weak cryptography, including words related to descriptions and design guidance. No evidence found of MD5, DES, hashing, encryption, credential handling, or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language writing instructions and examples. There is no code path or semantic evidence of cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged an error-message example, but the line only shows the phrase 'Invalid input' as bad UX copy. No evidence found of host, user, process, environment, network, or filesystem reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a user-facing copy example inside a UX-writing section. It contains no commands, APIs, or instructions to enumerate system information.

content.value_statement: Unclear interface text can make users hesitate, make mistakes, or contact support. This skill reviews UX copy and rewrites it into clear, specific, and helpful language.
actual_capabilities: Reviews interface copy for jargon, ambiguity, passive voice, missing context, and tone mismatch.
Improves error messages with plain-language explanations and recovery steps.
Rewrites form labels, placeholders, help text, buttons, and confirmation dialogs.
Creates clearer empty states, success messages, loading states, and navigation labels.
Checks copy for actionability, brevity, consistency, and audience fit.
limitations: It does not run usability tests with real users.
It depends on the context, audience, and product constraints provided by the user.
It does not enforce brand voice unless brand guidance is supplied.
It focuses on UX copy and does not redesign application behavior.

use_cases.title: Clarify Error Recovery
use_cases.description: Rewrite vague error messages so users know what happened and how to fix the problem.

use_cases.title: Improve Form Completion
use_cases.description: Make form labels, helper text, placeholders, and validation messages easier to understand.

use_cases.title: Standardize Product Language
use_cases.description: Review interface text for consistent terms, concise phrasing, and user-centered action labels.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Single Message
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one interface text string.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use clarify to improve this message: [paste message]. The audience is [audience]. The user is trying to [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Form
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of labels and instructions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use clarify to review this form copy: [paste labels, helper text, and errors]. Suggest clearer text for each field.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite a Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across multiple interface states.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use clarify to improve copy across this flow: [paste screens or states]. Include errors, empty states, success messages, and CTAs.

prompt_templates.title: Create Copy Standards
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced team guidance for repeated copy decisions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use clarify to create UX copy guidance for [product area]. Include rules for tone, buttons, errors, help text, and destructive actions.

output_examples.output: You do not have permission to view this page. Contact your admin for access.
No projects yet. Create your first project to get started.

best_practices: Provide the user goal, audience, emotional context, and any length limits before asking for rewrites.
Review copy in the full interface flow so terminology stays consistent across states.
Ask for alternatives when tone, legal constraints, or localization needs are still uncertain.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to polish text without explaining where users see the message.
Do not use placeholder text as the only label for a form field.
Do not accept clever or funny error text when users need clear recovery steps.

faq.question: What type of copy does this skill improve?
faq.answer: It improves UX copy such as errors, labels, buttons, help text, empty states, success messages, loading states, and confirmations.

faq.question: Can it write from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the product context, user goal, audience, and constraints so it can draft useful copy.

faq.question: Does it support brand voice?
faq.answer: Yes, if you provide brand voice rules or examples. Without them, it uses clear and neutral product language.

faq.question: Will it change interface design?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on the words in the interface, though it may point out when layout or behavior affects clarity.

faq.question: Is this useful for developers?
faq.answer: Yes. Developers can use it to improve labels, validation messages, empty states, and release-facing interface text.

faq.question: Does it require access to code?
faq.answer: No. It can work from pasted copy, screenshots described in text, product requirements, or interface state descriptions.
